description: John Zenon, a 42-year-old aerospace machinist, has developed a chronic dry cough and shortness of breath. Recent environmental air quality sampling in his sector revealed beryllium dust levels exceeding the OSHA Permissible Exposure Limit. A subsequent BeLPT and HRCT scan confirmed a diagnosis of Chronic Beryllium Disease (Berylliosis). As the Occupational Health Technician, complete the medical surveillance documentation in GNU Health. Record the Berylliosis diagnosis (ICD-10), create a clinical evaluation documenting his impaired respiratory status and oxygen saturation, order at least 2 baseline lab tests (e.g., CBC, BMP, or Immunology), prescribe a systemic corticosteroid (Prednisone or Dexamethasone) for lung inflammation, and schedule an occupational pulmonology follow-up appointment.
